We will review typical scenarios — voice input and search, call center automation, transcription and dialogue analysis, as well as voicing hints and statuses. We will discuss how the ASR/TTS pipeline is organized in production: data collection and annotation, model selection, quality metrics (WER/MOS), handling noise, accents and domain-specific vocabulary. We will show how latency and stability are ensured in real time, as well as how to integrate solutions into mobile and server products. We will separately address security and privacy of voice data and approaches to quality monitoring after release. Finally, we will share practical insights and directions for developing speech services in the X5 ecosystem.

talks.title: Improving Diarization Quality with End-to-End Models
talks.abstract: Diarization is a key stage in audio processing that determines the quality of transcriptions, analytics, and subsequent speech processing. In this talk, we will examine why classical pipelines "segmentation + embeddings + clustering" often produce errors on overlapping speech, noise, and short utterances, and how end-to-end approaches help reduce them. We will discuss architectural ideas of modern E2E diarization models, training methods on "who speaks when" annotations, and integration with ASR to obtain more stable speaker labels in transcripts. We will show which metrics and evaluation protocols to use for fair comparison, and which data/augmentations have the strongest impact on results. We will also address practical questions: computational requirements, latency in online mode, and typical failure points in production. As a result, you will receive a set of recommendations on how to select and implement E2E diarization in a real speech-to-text service.

talks.title: High-Load Speech Transcription System
talks.abstract: In this talk, we will explore how to design and deploy a high-load automatic speech recognition (ASR) system capable of processing large streams of audio in real-time and batch modes. We will discuss ASR model selection and integration, transcription quality requirements, and methods for measuring accuracy on production data. We will present a typical architecture: audio ingestion, queues and sharding, inference services, post-processing (punctuation, diarization, normalization), and result delivery. We will separately address scaling across CPU/GPU, latency optimization, caching, and compute cost management. We will cover reliability: graceful degradation, retries, idempotency, monitoring and alerting, as well as handling poor-quality audio and traffic spikes. Finally, we will provide practical recommendations for operations, testing, and secure audio data processing.

talks.title: Developing an End-to-End Pipeline for Speech Synthesis Research
talks.abstract: In this talk, we will discuss how to build an end-to-end pipeline for research experiments in speech synthesis — from data preparation and model training to automatic quality assessment and delivery of results to production. We will explore how to organize reproducibility: versioning of datasets, configurations, artifacts, and metrics, as well as fast iterations when changing architectures and features. We will show where approaches from recommender systems and predictive analytics are applicable in such a pipeline: voice personalization, user adaptation, and prediction of generation quality and errors. We will separately discuss the use of reinforcement learning to optimize subjective criteria (naturalness, intelligibility, style) and alignment with human preferences. We will address practical questions: metric selection, construction of offline/online experiments, and degradation control. As a result, you will receive a set of architectural and organizational solutions that accelerate research and make its results transferable to production.

talks.title: Reinforcement Learning for Recommendation System Optimization Using Non-Differentiable Ranking Metrics
talks.speakers: Халтурин А., Сайфуллин В., Чернов А.
talks.abstract: In recommendation systems, business objectives are often expressed through non-differentiable ranking metrics — NDCG, MAP, MRR, and others — which causes standard gradient-based training to optimize only proxy losses and lose connection with actual quality. In this talk, we will examine how to formulate the ranking task as a reinforcement learning problem: what to consider as state, action, and reward, and how to correctly set the reward for the target metric. We will discuss policy gradient and actor-critic approaches for training ranking policies, as well as methods for reducing variance and stabilizing learning with sparse rewards. We will also address practical issues: offline learning from logs, dealing with bias from the data collection policy, and safe online updates through A/B testing. Using examples, we will show when RL actually delivers improvements on target metrics and what trade-offs arise in latency, interpretability, and degradation risks.

talks.title: RL Environment for Training Agent Capabilities of Modern LLMs
talks.abstract: The talk presents an RL environment designed for training and evaluating agent capabilities of modern large language models. We will discuss which agent skills can be formalized as tasks: goal setting, planning, tool use, memory management, and safe action execution in an environment. We will show how scenarios and metrics are structured to measure progress not only by answer quality, but also by success of multi-step trajectories and robustness to errors. We will discuss how to organize reinforcement learning and feedback to reduce hallucinations in actions and increase the reliability of autonomous behavior. We will separately address reproducibility, simulation of external systems, and constraints important to consider when transferring results to real applications. As a result, you will gain practical understanding of how to build benches for agent-based LLM training and which experiments provide the greatest gains.

talks.title: GigaStudio
talks.abstract: In this talk, we will examine GigaStudio as a practical environment for working with LLMs: from prototyping to deployment in production scenarios. We will discuss how to collect and test prompts, manage context and response quality, as well as reduce costs and latency. We will review typical pipelines: RAG on internal data, tools (tools/function calling), agents and chain orchestration. We will separately address security: data leaks, prompt injection and access control to knowledge sources. We will show how to measure quality (evaluations, A/B testing, automatic metrics) and build an iteration process within a team. As a result, you will gain a set of approaches that allow you to launch LLM functions in real applications faster and more reliably.

talks.title: Exploring the Use of Generative Models for Assessing the Condition of Various Objects at Wildberries Pickup Points
talks.abstract: In this talk, we will examine how generative models can be applied to automated assessment of object conditions at Wildberries order pickup points. We will discuss the problem statement and data sources: photographs and video streams, typical damage and deviation scenarios, as well as requirements for processing quality and speed. We will show how to combine computer vision and GenAI for defect description, condition classification, and generation of clear reports for operational teams. We will explore approaches to model training and fine-tuning, work with annotations, and strategies for combating noise and domain shifts between pickup points. We will separately address validation, interpretability, and hallucination reduction in decision-making. Finally, we will compare results with classical CV pipelines and discuss where the generative approach delivers maximum impact in production.

talks.title: Development of a Service for Log Processing and Analysis Based on Artificial Intelligence
talks.abstract: In this talk, we will discuss the creation of a service that automatically collects, normalizes, and analyzes application logs using artificial intelligence models. We will examine how to extract events, parameters, and context from unstructured messages to accelerate incident root cause analysis and reduce alert noise. We will demonstrate approaches to clustering similar errors, anomaly detection, and building concise problem summaries for engineers. We will discuss the pipeline architecture: from ingestion and storage to training/inference and integrations with monitoring systems. We will separately address data quality, privacy, and secure handling of sensitive logs. Finally, we will share practical results, efficiency metrics, and the service development roadmap.

talks.title: Generative Service for Designers
talks.abstract: In this talk, we will discuss the creation of a generative service that helps designers quickly obtain variants of visual solutions and prototypes. We will examine how the combination of GenAI and computer vision enables understanding of source references, brand guidelines, and task context, and then generating consistent layouts and assets. We will discuss the pipeline: data preparation, model selection and fine-tuning, quality control, and protection against "hallucinations" in production. We will show how prompts and editing tools are structured so that the designer remains an author rather than an observer. We will separately discuss integration into familiar design processes and metrics by which we measured work acceleration and result stability. We will conclude with practical insights: where the service provides maximum effect and where generation still falls short of manual work.

talks.title: Paw to Paw.
talks.abstract: "Paw to Paw" is a social network that helps people find pets from shelters that best match their lifestyle and expectations. In this talk, we will explore how to combine questionnaire data, behavioral signals, and textual descriptions of animals into a unified matching model. We will show how NLP is used to normalize and extract semantic features from stories, medical notes, and volunteer comments to improve search and filtering quality. We will discuss the recommendation system: cold start, balance between "similar" and "new", as well as accounting for constraints (allergies, children, other pets, living conditions). We will separately address fairness and safety issues: how not to "hide" difficult animals and how to avoid toxic scenarios in the social part of the product. We will conclude with quality metrics and practical insights on how recommendations and language models can realistically increase the chances of successful pet adoption.

talks.title: Developing an MVP Prototype of an Intelligent AI Companion Toy for Children Aged 3–12
talks.abstract: In this talk, we will discuss the development of an MVP for an intelligent AI companion toy for children aged 3–12: from hypotheses and use case scenarios to the first working prototype. We will examine how to personalize dialogues, games, and educational tasks based on age, interests, and developmental dynamics using recommendation approaches and predictive analytics. We will show what data can be collected safely and with minimal sufficiency, how to build profiles and segments without overcomplicating the model, and how to evaluate recommendation quality under low-traffic conditions. We will discuss the pipeline architecture: events, features, models, online solutions, and feedback from children and parents. We will separately address reliability, content control, and prevention of unwanted recommendations. We will conclude with a list of metrics and readiness criteria for the MVP to move to pilot.

talks.title: Product Ranking and Candidate Generation in a Marketplace
talks.abstract: In this talk, we will examine how two key stages of marketplace search work: candidate generation and final product ranking. We will discuss what signals are used to understand queries and product cards, and how to combine textual and visual features to improve relevance. We will cover models for fast candidate selection (dual-tower embeddings, ANN search) and heavier ranking models that account for user context and business constraints. We will separately address cold start problems, long-tail inventory, and dealing with noise in click and purchase data. We will show approaches to offline/online quality evaluation, A/B testing, and degradation monitoring. By the end, you will have a practical understanding of how to build a scalable search pipeline that is simultaneously accurate, fast, and resilient to catalog changes.

talks.title: Designing a System Using Machine Learning Methods to Enhance Human Productivity
talks.abstract: In this talk, we will examine the design of a system that increases personal and team productivity through machine learning methods. We will show how a combination of NLP and autonomous agents can transform scattered tasks, emails, and notes into a structured action plan: from intent extraction and prioritization to automatic delegation and execution tracking. We will discuss the architecture: context collection, agent orchestration, memory and security rules, as well as integrations with calendar, task trackers, and corporate knowledge bases. We will separately consider quality—utility metrics, reduction of "hallucinations", explainability of recommendations, and user feedback loops. We will address privacy and data access issues to ensure the system helps without violating company boundaries and policies. Finally, we will demonstrate typical scenarios and practical steps to begin implementing such an assistant.

talks.title: ReqAssist
talks.abstract: ReqAssist is a tool that helps formulate and refine requirements using autonomous agents. In this talk, we will explore how agents gather context from stakeholders, identify gaps and contradictions, and then propose variants of user stories, acceptance criteria, and scenarios. We will discuss how to organize a question–answer–verification cycle so that the agent does not hallucinate and remains grounded in sources. We will present architectural patterns: agent role separation, memory, task planning, and quality control. We will separately address integration into familiar workflows—issue trackers, documentation, and CI—and metrics by which you can measure the benefit. Finally, we will discuss typical risks (security, access, accountability) and practices that enable safe adoption of ReqAssist in a team.

talks.title: MediAI — AI Mediator for Couples
talks.abstract: MediAI is an LLM-based assistant that helps couples conduct difficult conversations more calmly and productively. In this talk, we will explore how the "mediator" works: it asks clarifying questions, records each party's positions, highlights needs, and helps formulate agreements without mutual accusations. We will discuss use cases — from everyday conflicts to discussions about finances and boundaries — and how the assistant maintains a neutral tone and dialogue structure. We will also address security: conversation privacy, data minimization, and safeguards to avoid substituting therapy and providing dangerous advice. We will show how to design prompts and context so that the LLM does not "take sides" and correctly handles emotions. Finally, we will discuss quality metrics and testing approaches to ensure that MediAI truly reduces tension and increases the chances of reaching agreement.

talks.title: CV-helper
talks.abstract: CV-helper is an NLP-based tool that helps quickly improve resumes and cover letters for specific job openings. The solution is built on autonomous agents that analyze CV text, extract requirements from job descriptions, and suggest targeted edits: achievement formulations, structure, key skills, and metrics. The system identifies gaps and mismatches, suggests which projects and results should be highlighted, and helps remove filler and clichés. A separate agent handles readability checks and consistent style, while another adapts content for different roles and levels (junior/middle/senior). The talk covers agent architecture, text processing pipeline, before/after examples, as well as limitations and risks of automation when working with personal data.

talks.title: Kuvio
talks.abstract: In this talk, we will discuss the Kuvio project — the application of machine learning in fintech to improve solution quality and user experience. We will examine which business tasks ML solves: scoring, fraud detection, customer behavior prediction, and offer personalization. We will discuss data: sources, cleaning, handling leaks and biases, and how to build a reliable training and validation pipeline. We will pay special attention to model interpretability and regulatory requirements so that solutions can be explained and defended. We will show how to organize quality monitoring in production, respond to drift, and safely update models. We will conclude with practical insights on what actually delivers value in fintech and which mistakes most often eliminate the benefits of ML.

talks.title: Development of a Q&A System for Answering Questions on Risk Modeling Based on Central Bank Documentation
talks.abstract: In this talk, we will discuss the creation of a Q&A system that answers questions on risk modeling based on regulatory and methodological documentation from the Central Bank. We will examine how to organize a pipeline for extracting and updating sources so that answers remain verifiable and tied to specific document sections. We will show how NLP components and retrieval approaches help find relevant fragments, resolve term ambiguities, and maintain context for complex queries. We will separately discuss the role of autonomous agents: task decomposition into steps, clarifying questions to the user, completeness verification, and self-control of answer quality. We will address compliance issues: citation, traceability, limitations on "hallucinations," and safe generation modes. Finally, we will share practical quality metrics and typical errors that arise when working with regulatory texts.

talks.title: Self-service AI-powered analytics
talks.abstract: The talk focuses on building self-service analytics where business users get answers to their questions without involving analysts and engineers. We will explore how AI helps translate natural language queries into metrics, segments, and reports, while lowering the barrier to entry for working with data. We will discuss quality aspects: result validation, explainability, access control, and preventing "hallucinations" at the data and semantic layer levels. We will present a typical solution architecture — from data sources and data marts to chat interfaces and usage monitoring. We will discuss which scenarios deliver maximum impact (ad-hoc questions, insight discovery, automation of regular reporting) and where limitations remain. Finally, we will provide practical recommendations for implementation and metrics to evaluate the success of the self-service approach.
